Game.Name: Date Kimiko no Virtual Tennis (Japan)
Game.plot: Overview:Date Kimiko no Virtual Tennis is a tennis game featuring and licensed by 1994 Australian Open semi-finalist Kimiko Date and is one of a handful of tennis sims for the Super Famicom to feature scaling Mode 7 graphics, similar to David Crane's Amazing Tennis. It was developed by Jorudan, better known in Japan for their Hisshou 777 Fighter pachinko series, and published by B-Ai, a subsidiary of Bandai.In addition to one-off Exhibition and Tournament modes, the player can take part in a World Tour that covers various global tournaments as well as passively watch advanced tennis strategies with the Tactics mode.Cheat: World No. 1World Tour mode... enter "Nice to meet you Take Kimiko Tate Kimiko Tate Kimiko" (in Japanese!) as password (see the screenshots for more details)... You will start the game ranking World No. 1. However, since you are injured, you will eventually lose that status. But no worries!
Game.Notes: Date Kimiko no Virtual Tennis is a Sports game, developed by Jorudan and published by B.I., which was released in Japan in 1994.
